title: Cleansing of Korriban

{{Quote|They need this final hunt done quickly and quietly… and we're the ''best''.|Guun Han Saresh|<ref name="shadows" />}}
In [[3993 BBY]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]], [[Shaela Nuur]], and [[Guun Han Saresh]] were sent on a '''secret mission''' to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] [[Terentatek/Legends|terentateks]] on the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Having established themselves as the best hunters in the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] in the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]] of previous [[Standard month/Legends|months]], the three [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] were specifically handpicked by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] to undertake this task. After journeying to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], the group began its investigation in the [[Dreshdae]] settlement. However, before they fought a single terentatek, Saresh left the group, disapproving of the budding romance between Qel-Droma and Nuur. Without their compatriot, Qel-Droma and Nuur proceeded onward into the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], where they believed the terentateks were living.

Venturing into a [[Shyrack cave|cave]], Qel-Droma and Nuur eventually came across a terentatek. Although Qel-Droma planned to [[Mind trick/Legends|confuse]] the beast with a mental [[Force power/Legends|Force power]], he realized that he could not make contact with the terentatek and was [[Death/Legends|killed]] before he could react. Consumed with grief and anger at the death of her lover, Nuur chased the terentatek deeper into the cave, eventually coming across the beast in the ancient [[Tomb of Naga Sadow|tomb]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]. There, she was attacked by another terentatek and was killed.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|After all, Duron, Shaela and Guun Han are our greatest hunters… ''together'', they can resist the dark side.|[[Aleco Stusea]]|<ref name="shadows" />}}
[[File:Jediassembly.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Duron Qel-Droma, Shaela Nuur, and Guun Han Saresh are given their mission.]]
In [[3996 BBY]], the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] led by [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lords]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] and [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] drew to a close, with a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] defeat. Although the Sith [[Army/Legends|armies]] had been bested, many Sith-[[Sith alchemy/Legends|created]] monstrosities still survived on [[Planet/Legends|planets]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], posing a threat to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the general populace. In response, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] organized the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]], a collaborative [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] effort aimed at eliminating these beasts wherever they trod. During the Hunt, a Jedi posse including [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Duron Qel-Droma]], [[Shaela Nuur]], and [[Guun Han Saresh]] garnered considerable renown as the very best the Order had to offer. The group traveled to worlds such as [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], and [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], focusing on battling [[Terentatek/Legends|terentateks]]<ref name="shadows" />—vicious, highly dangerous creations of Kun that fed off [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Blood/Legends|blood]].<ref name="nec" />

After a successful Jedi [[Cleansing of Tatooine|mission]] to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], an official end was called to the Great Hunt. However, the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] handpicked Qel-Droma, Nuur, and Saresh to undertake a secret mission. The Jedi would travel to the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a planet deeply steeped in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], and eliminate terentateks that were believed to exist on the world. Together, it was believed that they would be able to resist Korriban's dark lure. Once on Korriban, the three Jedi stopped in the [[Dreshdae]] settlement and began their investigation into the whereabouts of the terentateks. However, the group became strained, as Saresh disapproved of the budding romance between Qel-Droma and Nuur. Feeling that their relationship put the mission in danger, Saresh left the group in order to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] a "great beast" he had heard lived in the [[Shadowlands/Legends|Shadowlands]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. Before he departed, Saresh gave Qel-Droma and Nuur a [[Keycard/Legends|keycard]] that would grant them access to the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]]—where they believed the terentateks were—leaving his compatriots to take the beasts on by themselves.<ref name="shadows" />

==The cleansing==
{{Quote|I can't make contact…|Duron Qel-Droma, immediately before his death|<ref name="shadows" />}}
[[File:ShaelaDurondead.jpg|thumb|right|175px|Duron Qel-Droma lies dead in Shaela Nuur's arms.]]
Three [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after Saresh's abrupt departure, Qel-Droma and Nuur ventured forth into the Valley of the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lords]]. As they approached a [[Shyrack cave|cave]], the two Jedi [[Force sense/Legends|sensed]] something through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]—they realized that, at that moment, Saresh had [[Death/Legends|died]] in his pursuit on Kashyyyk. Although disturbed by the loss of their friend and former teammate, Qel-Droma and Nuur pressed on into the cave. Together, the hunters slew several lesser beasts, including [[Shyrack/Legends|shyrack]] and [[Kinrath/Legends|kinrath]], as they progressed farther into the caverns. Finally, at long last, they encountered a terentatek. Although Qel-Droma planned to use [[Mind trick/Legends|mental]] [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] to confuse the beast while Nuur attacked it up close, he realized to his horror that he could not make mental contact with their target. Before he could react, the terentatek tossed Nuur aside and grabbed Qel-Droma in one of its large claws, fatally wounding him.<ref name="shadows" />

Nuur rushed to her fallen lover in grief, but Qel-Droma reassured her—as he lay dying, the Jedi had a prophetic [[Force vision/Legends|vision]], showing him that a Jedi named [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] would eventually find his body and use his [[Qel-Droma robe|robe]] to help him defeat a [[Darth Malak|Sith Lord]]. After relaying this hopeful message to Nuur, Qel-Droma died. Nuur was left alone and was filled with rage at her lover's death. Her mind set on vengeance, Nuur set out after the wounded terentatek, following the cave system into the ancient buried [[Tomb of Naga Sadow|tomb]] of Dark Lord of the Sith [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]. Within, she found the injured beast but was surprised by a healthy terentatek that was hidden in the shadows. Before she could engage either creature, her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] flickered and and deactivated as the [[Solari crystal/Legends|Solari]] crystal within reacted to her betrayal of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]].<ref name="shadows" /> Nuur was killed by the terentateks<ref name="nec" /> as she remembered [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ood Bnar]]'s warning to always stay in the light.<ref name="shadows" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|The Council felt it was too dangerous to send anyone else in search of their remains, so their exact fate is not known. But let their deaths serve as a warning to you, Padawan. The terentatek are an abomination, they are ruthless engines of death and destruction. The Council had no choice but to destroy them, for the greater good of the galaxy. There is not much I can tell you other than their names: Duron Qel-Droma, Guun Han Saresh and Shaela Nuur. They disappeared nearly forty years ago, long before my time at the Academy.|[[Deesra Luur Jada]], to Revan, years after Qel-Droma's death|<ref name="kotor">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
Although the Jedi Council was relatively certain that Qel-Droma and Nuur had perished on Korriban, due to the danger inherent in sending someone to find their bodies, their exact fate remained uncertain.<ref name="kotor" /> In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="nec" /> Qel-Droma's body was discovered by Revan, during the Jedi's quest to find the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]]s—as Qel-Droma had predicted in his dying words, Revan took the Qel-Droma [[Clothing/Legends|robe]] and soon afterward [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeated]] the Sith Lord [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Malak.<ref name="kotor" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Great Hunt skirmish on Korriban was first mentioned in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''.<ref name="kotor" /> The event first appeared in "[[Shadows and Light]]," a story published in [[Star Wars Tales 23|''Star Wars Tales'' 23]], that was written by [[Joshua Ortega]], illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]],<ref name="shadows" /> and released on [[May 11]], [[2005]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54696/star_wars_tales_1999_23|text=Star Wars Tales (1999) #23}}</ref>
